title: Multi-Brand Gift Cards
description: What are the most flexible, widely accepted multi-brand gift card options in France that can be used across fashion, beauty, home, and leisure retailers both online and in-store?
narrative: The user is seeking gift card solutions that offer maximum versatility and convenience, allowing the recipient to choose from a broad network of retailers across multiple categories (e.g., fashion, beauty, home, leisure) both online and in physical stores. Relevance is determined by whether the gift card is multi-brand (accepted by many different retailers), nationally available in France, and usable across diverse consumer sectors. Single-brand gift cards (e.g., Decathlon, Bonobo, Au Vieux Campeur) are not relevant, even if they are digital or flexible within their own ecosystem. The ideal solution should also support accumulation of value, have broad merchant participation (e.g., 500+ brands), and be usable for both personal and professional gifting purposes.

Relevance is determined by documents that explain the eligibility criteria (e.g., minimum 6 months of recent work, 30% increase in total entitlement, or daily allowance ≤ €20), the procedural steps (21-day reflection period, written application to Pôle Emploi), and the irreversible consequences of choosing this option. The user is likely a home cook or food enthusiast looking to move beyond traditional myths and adopt scientifically informed practices. Relevance is determined by empirical testing, nutritional analysis, or detailed culinary reasoning—not general advice, recipes without analysis, or unrelated topics like potato cultivation or sprout toxicity.

title: Return-to-Work Bonus
description: What are the eligibility criteria and application process for a return-to-work bonus in France after resuming employment following a period of social assistance?
narrative: The user is seeking clarification on whether they qualify for a return-to-work bonus after securing a job following receipt of RSA, SSA, or similar social benefits. They need to understand the legal requirements—such as minimum contract duration (CDI/CDD), hours worked (e.g., 78 hours/month), and timing (e.g., 1 or 4 months)—as well as which agency to contact (CAF, Pôle Emploi, or General Council). Relevance is determined by documents that specify eligibility conditions, application procedures, payment amounts (e.g., €1,000), and administrative nuances like regional variations (APRE) or program discontinuation dates (e.g., PEP abolished in 2016). Documents discussing fraud concerns, conflicting advice, or unrelated bonuses (e.g., Christmas or Macron bonus) are not relevant.
